body:

ERP Software for Builders & Developers
From BOQ to client handover, SiteFlow connects every stage of your building project — budget control, procurement, subcontractor billing, and live P&L in one place.
Problems SiteFlow Solves
✕
BOQ made once, never tracked against actual spend
✕
Material over-ordering and wastage across units
✕
Subcontractor billing disputes with no linked progress data

ERP Software for EPC Companies
Engineering, Procurement, and Construction connected through a single BOQ — so every purchase order traces to a scope item, every site update reflects against your contracted value, and budget overruns are caught before they become committed costs.
Problems SiteFlow Solves
✕
POs raised without checking BOQ budget availability
✕
Multi-site progress and cost tracked in disconnected spreadsheets
✕
Subcontractor bills approved without verified installed quantities

/outer FAQ
FAQ
Questions About SiteFlow ERP for Your Construction Segment
Yes. SiteFlow is used by builders and developers, civil contractors, interior firms, and infrastructure and EPC companies across India and the Middle East. The platform is the same — features are configured based on how your business type actually operates. A civil contractor uses GPS attendance and quality checklists daily; an interior firm uses quotations and scope management. Both run on SiteFlow.
Not at all. SiteFlow is built to go live in under a day with no IT team. Our onboarding manager loads your data and trains your team. Interior companies typically use SiteFlow for quotations, scope tracking, vendor billing, and client invoicing — you can start with just those modules and expand as you grow. Our smallest clients manage 2 projects; our largest manage 50+.
Yes. SiteFlow is designed for multi-site, multi-project operations. You get a consolidated dashboard showing progress, cost, attendance, and billing across all sites simultaneously. This is especially used by infrastructure and EPC companies managing road, pipeline, and metro projects across different locations. Material libraries, rate libraries, and party masters are all shared centrally.
SiteFlow links every subcontractor bill to actual measured progress — not just what the subcontractor claims. Work orders are created with quantities and rates. Bills are raised against completed work only. Multi-level approval workflows ensure no bill moves forward without the right sign-offs. Retention amounts are tracked automatically and released at defined milestones. This eliminates overpayment disputes entirely.
Site engineers who have never used software are entering DPRs in SiteFlow within their first week. The mobile app is optimised for on-site use — minimal taps, works on any Android phone, and has offline capability. We provide module-wise tutorials in Hindi and English. Your onboarding manager stays available for the first 30 days. WhatsApp remains the primary alert and notification channel for field teams — no new app habits required.
Yes. Infrastructure clients use SiteFlow for activity scheduling, BOQ-linked progress tracking, production management (concrete batching, asphalt mixes), equipment tracking, and subcontractor billing tied to measured quantities. Road, railway, pipeline, and solar projects are all supported. Clients like SKC Infra and Alamdar Infrastructure run large EPC contracts on SiteFlow today.
Yes — and this is one of the most common setups. Finance stays on Tally; site execution runs on SiteFlow. Purchase orders, vendor invoices, and client receipts sync both ways automatically. GST ledgers, cost centres, and narration formats are configured to match your existing Tally setup. Zoho Books integration is also available. Zero double entry for your accounts team.
Final CTA
